steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence reads: "...a metrical foot ______ to in English metrical verse as a trochee." This phrase follows a comma after an independent clause, making it an appositive noun phrase that elaborates on "one stressed syllable followed by one unstressed syllable." Inside a noun phrase, the verb slot must be filled by a participial adjective (past or present participle), not a finite verb — a finite verb would create a second independent clause joined only by a comma, which is a comma splice.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — referred: past participle used as an adjective within the noun phrase: "a metrical foot referred to in English metrical verse as a trochee." Grammatically correct. ✓
  • Choice B — is referred: finite verb form. Creates "a metrical foot is referred to..." — a full independent clause, producing a comma splice with the preceding clause. ✗
  • Choice C — referring: present participle. "Refer to" is not used in the active voice here; "a metrical foot referring to itself as a trochee" is idiomatically wrong — the foot is referred to by others. ✗
  • Choice D — refers: finite verb form. Creates a comma splice, same problem as Choice B — is referred. Also idiomatically wrong for the same reason as Choice C — referring. ✗
That matches Choice A — referred.
